name: Blueberry
summary: A small berry packed with anthocyanins — the blue-purple pigments studied for memory, brain aging, and blood-vessel function.
superfoodReason: One of the highest-anthocyanin common fruits, with randomized human trials linking it to better memory and vascular function.

benefits.mechanism: Blueberry anthocyanins cross into the brain and reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in regions tied to memory, and may improve blood flow there. In a trial of older adults with early memory decline, daily blueberry improved memory measures.

citations.title: Blueberry supplementation improves memory in older adults (Krikorian et al., J Agric Food Chem 2010)

benefits.mechanism: The same polyphenols improve endothelial function — how well the lining of your arteries dilates — which supports healthy circulation and blood pressure. A double-blind RCT in older adults found wild-blueberry polyphenols improved vascular function.

citations.title: Wild blueberry (poly)phenols can improve vascular function and cognitive performance in healthy older individuals: a double-blind randomized controlled trial (Wood et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2023)

surprisingFacts.text: Wild (lowbush) blueberries are smaller than cultivated ones but pack noticeably more anthocyanin per gram — the pigment is concentrated in the skin, and wild berries have more skin per volume.
